mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a broad contraction in consumer demand and credit-dependent spending, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields a positive 60-day forward return in > 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production rising above its 12-month moving average confirms strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above the 12-month average coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases during recovery phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved earnings trajectories.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year sample, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of periods following CPIAUCSL MoM spikes > 0.4%, or the signal fails to predict direction during low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved consumer confidence.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index signals weakening manufacturing demand and inventory drawdowns, which forecasts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of late-cycle observations, or the mechanism loses predictive accuracy when FEDFUNDS drops below 0.5%.
